---
title: "Results"
---

## Key findings (draft)
Add bullet points as you go.

## Figures
Add figures as you generate them. Example:

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(pheatmap)
library(dplyr)

data <- read_excel("data/GSE61271_gene_names_and_GSM_headers.xlsx")
metadata <- read.csv("data/GSE61271_sample_metadata.csv")

cat("1. Initial data rows:", nrow(data), "\n")
cat("   Data columns:", ncol(data), "\n")

data$Gene <- as.character(data$Gene)
data$Gene[is.na(data$Gene) | data$Gene == ""] <- paste0("Unknown_", which(is.na(data$Gene) | data$Gene == ""))

data <- data[!grepl("^ENSSSCG", data$Gene, ignore.case = TRUE), ]
cat("2. After removing ENSSSCG rows:", nrow(data), "\n")

rownames(data) <- make.unique(data$Gene)

data$Gene <- NULL
data$Gene_name <- NULL

cat("3. Columns before numeric conversion:", ncol(data), "\n")

data[] <- lapply(data, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x)))
data <- data[complete.cases(data), ]
cat("4. After numeric conversion & removing NAs:", nrow(data), "rows\n")

row_var <- apply(data, 1, var, na.rm = TRUE)
cat("5. Variance stats:\n")
print(summary(row_var))

data <- data[row_var > 0, ]
cat("6. After variance > 0 filter:", nrow(data), "rows\n")

gene_variance <- apply(data, 1, var, na.rm = TRUE)
n_genes <- min(50, nrow(data))
cat("7. Top genes to select:", n_genes, "\n")

if(n_genes < 2) {
  cat("\n⚠️ WARNING: Only", n_genes, "genes available!\n")
  cat("Showing all available genes.\n")
  top_genes <- rownames(data)
} else {
  top_genes <- names(sort(gene_variance, decreasing = TRUE))[1:n_genes]
}

heatmap_data <- data[top_genes, ]
cat("8. Final heatmap matrix:", nrow(heatmap_data), "genes x", ncol(heatmap_data), "samples\n")
cat("   Matrix is finite:", all(is.finite(as.matrix(heatmap_data))), "\n")

metadata2 <- metadata[match(colnames(heatmap_data), metadata$geo_accession), ]

annotation_col <- data.frame(
  BiologicalState = metadata2$biological_state,
  Sex = metadata2$Sex
)

rownames(annotation_col) <- colnames(heatmap_data)

cluster_rows <- nrow(heatmap_data) >= 2
cluster_cols <- ncol(heatmap_data) >= 2

cat("9. Clustering - rows:", cluster_rows, ", cols:", cluster_cols, "\n")

pheatmap(
  as.matrix(heatmap_data),
  scale = "none",
  cluster_rows = cluster_rows,  
  cluster_cols = cluster_cols,  
  annotation_col = annotation_col,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  angle_col = 45,
  fontsize_col = 7,
  main = "Top 50 Most Variable Genes",
  width = 10,
  height = 8
)

```
